commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f284dcd2032692e611a3bd677c14d1a23b0ccd2f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds OpenCode Zen provider runtime hooks for dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, base URL normalization, manifest runtimeAugment metadata, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +179, Tests +144. Total +323 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lacks the requested OpenCode Zen dynamic/catalog hooks, and the PR's normalization and static catalog behavior are visible in the proposed source. I did not run tests or a live OpenCode request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/maintainer follow-up for real proof and provider-routing compatibility, not a safe automation-only repair on this external PR.

target.title: feat(opencode): add resolveDynamicModel and augmentModelCatalog hooks to Zen plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- adds OpenCode Zen dynamic model resolution for unregistered upstream model ids
- adds catalog augmentation so Zen-only OpenCode models show up without manual `models.providers` config
- keeps existing OpenCode plugin/provider boundary intact

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Zen plugin can resolve and advertise OpenCode models like `deepseek-v4-flash-free` and `claude-opus-4-6` without hand-maintained model config.

Real environment tested: macOS checkout of this PR branch, Node/pnpm repo toolchain, dependencies installed from the lockfile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'import plugin from "./extensions/opencode/index.ts"; const providers=[]; const api={registerProvider:(p)=>providers.push(p),registerMediaUnderstandingProvider:()=>{}}; plugin.register(api); const provider=providers[0]; const deep=provider.resolveDynamicModel({modelId:"deepseek-v4-flash-free",modelRegistry:{find:()=>null}}); const opus=provider.resolveDynamicModel({modelId:"claude-opus-4-6",modelRegistry:{find:()=>null}}); const entries=await provider.augmentModelCatalog({env:{},entries:[]}); console.log(JSON.stringify({provider:provider.id,deepseek:deep?.id,opus:opus?.id,catalogContainsDeepseek:entries.some((entry)=>entry.id==="deepseek-v4-flash-free"),catalogContainsOpus:entries.some((entry)=>entry.id==="claude-opus-4-6"),catalogSize:entries.length},null,2));'
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm test -- extensions/opencode -- --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
node --import tsx ...
{
  "provider": "opencode",
  "deepseek": "deepseek-v4-flash-free",
  "opus": "claude-opus-4-6",
  "catalogContainsDeepseek": true,
  "catalogContainsOpus": true,
  "catalogSize": 5
}

pnpm check:test-types
OpenClaw Plugin Extension Tests Typecheck
...completed with exit 0

pnpm test -- extensions/opencode -- --reporter=verbose
[ERROR] fetch failed
For help, run: pnpm help run
```

Observed result after fix: the OpenCode Zen plugin runtime hook resolves both target dynamic models and advertises them from catalog augmentation; the type lane that was failing on `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` also passes after updating the hook test contexts for the current SDK contract.

What was not tested: no live OpenCode API call was run because this environment has no OpenCode key available; the focused Vitest command was blocked by pnpm fetch in this checkout.

## Verification

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check:test-types
```
target.author: olisikh

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
